qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated upload permits arbitrary file write and destination traversal
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about project import accepting attacker-controlled project scripts and later executing them without sandboxing. This finding is a separate `/api/upload` arbitrary-write and traversal bug and does not involve imported `scripts[]`, project import, or the advisory RCE path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Network-injected log content is rendered as HTML in an Electron renderer with Node integration, enabling XSS-to-RCE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a log-view `[innerHTML]` sink in the Electron client reached through request logging. It does not use project import or the imported-project script storage and execution path described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled project `svgcontent` is injected into an unsafe Electron renderer, enabling local XSS-to-RCE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns `view.svgcontent` being injected with `innerHTML` in the Electron client. The advisory root cause is imported-project `scripts[]` being accepted through `server/api/projects/index.js` and executed unsandboxed, which is a different source-to-sink path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's imported-project script execution root cause. The advisory describes project import storing attacker-controlled `scripts[]` and later executing `script.code` without sandboxing; the surviving qualifying findings cover `/api/upload` traversal and Electron HTML injection instead.
evidence: The GHSA/CVE description says project import allows RCE because imported project files contain user-supplied scripts that are not sanitized or sandboxed.
`server/api/projects/index.js` imports project bodies through `POST /api/project` by passing `req.body` into `runtime.project.setProject(req.body)` and restarting the runtime.

`server/runtime/project/index.js` persists attacker-controlled `scripts[]` entries from imported projects, and `server/runtime/scripts/msm.js` later turns `script.code` into JavaScript functions and compiles them with `Module._compile`.

None of the three final qualifying findings references `POST /api/project`, imported `scripts[]`, or `server/runtime/scripts/msm.js`; the closest accepted finding instead targets `view.svgcontent` DOM injection in `client/src/app/fuxa-view/fuxa-view.component.ts`.
